channel.title: Orthodox Christian Sermons: Feasts of the Savior, Theophany
channel.description: Audio Orthodox Christian Sermons: Feasts of the Savior, Theophany

item.title: Sunday before Theophany, The "beginning" of the Gospel is accomplished every day. , 2009
item.description: :Sunday before Theophany. The "beginning" of the Gospel is accomplished every day. , 2009
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Sunday before Theophany. The "beginning" of the Gospel is accomplished every day. , 2009

item.title: Sunday before Theophany, Prepare Ye The Way Of The Lord Make His Paths Straight, 2011-01-10
item.description: :Sunday before Theophany. On the Sunday before Theophany we read from the beginning of the Gospel of Mark 2 important prophesies regarding the ministry of Jesus Christ, and regarding the ministry of John the Baptist. The "baptism of John", and of Christ, and our own is explained, and the admonition of St John: "Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight", which applies to each one of us, all the time, is explained. There is a very important nuance of meaning that is missed by too many Christians. Our life must be a continual attempt to obey this admonition, and without our effort to do so, we cannot be saved, while, on the other hand, our success in fulfilling this command does not determine if we are saved either. This is the old faith vs. works argument, which, for those who were not raised in the church, can be very confusing. It is actually quite easy to understand (after you have understood it!). Our baptism, and all that God does to give us His grace, enables us to become perfected and to know Him. We are responsible for trying, and only because of His help, we will succeed. Overall, if our life is an effort to fulfill this commandment, we will be saved.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Sunday before Theophany. On the Sunday before Theophany we read from the beginning of the Gospel of Mark 2 important prophesies regarding the ministry of Jesus Christ, and regarding the ministry of John the Baptist. The "baptism of John", and of Christ, and our own is explained, and the admonition of St John: "Prepare ye the way of the Lord, make his paths straight", which applies to each one of us, all the time, is explained. There is a very important nuance of meaning that is missed by too many Christians. Our life must be a continual attempt to obey this admonition, and without our effort to do so, we cannot be saved, while, on the other hand, our success in fulfilling this command does not determine if we are saved either. This is the old faith vs. works argument, which, for those who were not raised in the church, can be very confusing. It is actually quite easy to understand (after you have understood it!). Our baptism, and all that God does to give us His grace, enables us to become perfected and to know Him. We are responsible for trying, and only because of His help, we will succeed. Overall, if our life is an effort to fulfill this commandment, we will be saved.

item.title: Sunday before Theophany, 2012-01-15
item.description: :Sunday before Theophany. The Sunday before Theophany (the baptism of Jesus Christ), we read from Mark about the "beginning of the Gospel of Jesus Christ" and about the baptism of John, which is a vivid type of Christian Baptism. The entire selection, form Mark 1:1-8 us explained, and especially how John's baptism (of repentance) is different (and the same) as Christian baptism. It is also appropriate on this day to think about the eventual fruit of baptism, which is human perfection and union with God, and the passage of 2Timothy 4:5-8 presents us with a beautiful "after" picture. This is our destiny, if we accept baptism and work to attain it.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Sunday before Theophany. The Sunday before Theophany (the baptism of Jesus Christ), we read from Mark about the "beginning of the Gospel of Jesus Christ" and about the baptism of John, which is a vivid type of Christian Baptism. The entire selection, form Mark 1:1-8 us explained, and especially how John's baptism (of repentance) is different (and the same) as Christian baptism. It is also appropriate on this day to think about the eventual fruit of baptism, which is human perfection and union with God, and the passage of 2Timothy 4:5-8 presents us with a beautiful "after" picture. This is our destiny, if we accept baptism and work to attain it.

item.title: Sunday before Theophany, 2014-01-18
item.description: :Sunday before Theophany. What happened at the baptism of Christ? The pre-festival troparion explains it. Why did the Theophany (appearance the Holy Triniy) occur at the baptism of Christ? This is central to the purpose of the reason Christ was baptized, and how we are made - we differ from animals and angels in a critical way, which is mystically described at the baptism of Christ.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Sunday before Theophany. What happened at the baptism of Christ? The pre-festival troparion explains it. Why did the Theophany (appearance the Holy Triniy) occur at the baptism of Christ? This is central to the purpose of the reason Christ was baptized, and how we are made - we differ from animals and angels in a critical way, which is mystically described at the baptism of Christ.

item.title: Sunday before Theophany, 2015-01-18
item.description: :Sunday before Theophany. The Gospel read on the Sunday before Theophany helps us to prepare for the feast. This we must do and redo every day. We discuss the difference in how Orthodox Christians prepare for feasts. There was a messenger that heralded the coming of Christ, and we also have such a messenger - the conscience. We must learn to cultivate and listen to this messenger.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Sunday before Theophany. The Gospel read on the Sunday before Theophany helps us to prepare for the feast. This we must do and redo every day. We discuss the difference in how Orthodox Christians prepare for feasts. There was a messenger that heralded the coming of Christ, and we also have such a messenger - the conscience. We must learn to cultivate and listen to this messenger.

item.title: Theophany (Baptism of the Lord), The Grace Of God That Bringeth Salvation Hath Appeared To All Men, 2011-01-19
item.description: :Theophany (Baptism of the Lord). Explanation of the reason for the Lord's baptism, including the prophesies about the visible changes in the Jordan when the Lord was baptised.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Theophany (Baptism of the Lord). Explanation of the reason for the Lord's baptism, including the prophesies about the visible changes in the Jordan when the Lord was baptised.

item.itunes:summary: :Theophany (Baptism of the Lord). Synaxarion For Theophany, 2012-01-18

item.title: Theophany (Baptism of the Lord), That He Might Redeem Us From All Iniquity And Purify Unto Himself A Peculiar People Zealous Of Good Works, 2012-01-19
item.description: :Theophany (Baptism of the Lord). The epistle for Theophany, the Baptism of the Lord, describes for us the purpose of baptism, and especially its "moral dogma". What is this? Most people know that Christianity rests upon various dogmas about God, such as that God is a Trinity of persons, and that the Son of God, Jesus Christ is God and man, but many do not know as well our moral dogmas. The Epistle to Titus describes both the dogma what Christian baptism does. and also the moral dogma concerning it - what we must do. If we do not understnad and embrace this dogma we cannot be saved.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Theophany (Baptism of the Lord). The epistle for Theophany, the Baptism of the Lord, describes for us the purpose of baptism, and especially its "moral dogma". What is this? Most people know that Christianity rests upon various dogmas about God, such as that God is a Trinity of persons, and that the Son of God, Jesus Christ is God and man, but many do not know as well our moral dogmas. The Epistle to Titus describes both the dogma what Christian baptism does. and also the moral dogma concerning it - what we must do. If we do not understnad and embrace this dogma we cannot be saved.

item.title: Sunday after Theophany, The Measure Of The Gift Of Christ, 2011-01-23
item.description: :Sunday after Theophany. Exegesis of the epistle read for the Sunday after Theophany, Ephesians 4:7-13. Why is this epistle read? What is "the measure of the gift of Christ." "He led captivity captive" describes what Christ's death and resurrection did for us, and is a way of understanding the effects of sin.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Sunday after Theophany. Exegesis of the epistle read for the Sunday after Theophany, Ephesians 4:7-13. Why is this epistle read? What is "the measure of the gift of Christ." "He led captivity captive" describes what Christ's death and resurrection did for us, and is a way of understanding the effects of sin.

item.title: Sunday after Theophany, 2012-01-21
item.description: :Sunday after Theophany. The selection read on the Sunday after Theophany (Ephesians 4:7-13) is critical to understand in detail. It contains the important dogmas abuot Christ's descent into Hell, and the rarely understood dogma whoch I will express as a question: "What is the purpose of the church?" It is critical to understand the purpose if our life, and of the church, and how baptism affects both. The exegesis of this Epistle explains this. This is a very important 9 minutes for a Christian to understand.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Sunday after Theophany. The selection read on the Sunday after Theophany (Ephesians 4:7-13) is critical to understand in detail. It contains the important dogmas abuot Christ's descent into Hell, and the rarely understood dogma whoch I will express as a question: "What is the purpose of the church?" It is critical to understand the purpose if our life, and of the church, and how baptism affects both. The exegesis of this Epistle explains this. This is a very important 9 minutes for a Christian to understand.

item.title: Sunday after Theophany, Saturday & Sunday after Theophany, 2012-01-22
item.description: :Sunday after Theophany. The history of what Jesus did after His baptism is critical for us to understand. He set the example, both by accepting baptism and showing us what we MUST do after baptism. Let' look in detail at how to encounter temptation, which will surely come to us. We must be ready.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Sunday after Theophany. The history of what Jesus did after His baptism is critical for us to understand. He set the example, both by accepting baptism and showing us what we MUST do after baptism. Let' look in detail at how to encounter temptation, which will surely come to us. We must be ready.

item.title: Sunday after Theophany, Sunday After Theophany, 2013-01-20
item.description: :Sunday after Theophany. The Epistle for the Sunday after Theophany, although it does not mention the word, is all about baptism. St Paul teaches us what our Lord's baptism did for us, and its end result - perfection. We look at this passage in detail, with its hopeful and intricate theology, which urges us to not be afraid to struggle for perfection!
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Sunday after Theophany. The Epistle for the Sunday after Theophany, although it does not mention the word, is all about baptism. St Paul teaches us what our Lord's baptism did for us, and its end result - perfection. We look at this passage in detail, with its hopeful and intricate theology, which urges us to not be afraid to struggle for perfection!

item.title: Sunday after Theophany, 2013-11-07
item.description: :Sunday after Theophany. Ephesians 4:7-13 is a virtual primer on some of the most import Christological subjects. It is read on the Sunday after Theophany, and also for certain Saints in the Menaion, such As Oct 25 (Marcian & Martyrius, notaries). In this short passage we learn of the descent into Hades, and see salvation, Unity and being truly human defined properly. St Paul is expressing thoughts dear to all Eastern Orthodox Christians, and sometimes not understood so well in the West. We look at these amazing verses: "Every one of us is given grace according to the measure of the gift of Christ" "He led captivity captive" "Till we all come in the unity of the faith, and of the knowledge of the Son of God, unto a perfect man, unto the measure of the stature of the fullness of Christ"
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Sunday after Theophany. Ephesians 4:7-13 is a virtual primer on some of the most import Christological subjects. It is read on the Sunday after Theophany, and also for certain Saints in the Menaion, such As Oct 25 (Marcian & Martyrius, notaries). In this short passage we learn of the descent into Hades, and see salvation, Unity and being truly human defined properly. St Paul is expressing thoughts dear to all Eastern Orthodox Christians, and sometimes not understood so well in the West. We look at these amazing verses: "Every one of us is given grace according to the measure of the gift of Christ" "He led captivity captive" "Till we all come in the unity of the faith, and of the knowledge of the Son of God, unto a perfect man, unto the measure of the stature of the fullness of Christ"

item.title: Sunday after Theophany, 2014-01-26
item.description: :Sunday after Theophany. Why this reading from Ephesians on the Sunday after Theophany? It contains the end result of baptism, and one of the best "definition" of salvation: unto a perfect man, unto the measure of the stature of the fulness of Christ". It also has marvelous Christology that many have never heard of, even in the church. Let us marvel at it, and be strengthened in our struggles.
item.itunes:author: Priest Seraphim Holland
item.itunes:summary: :Sunday after Theophany. Why this reading from Ephesians on the Sunday after Theophany? It contains the end result of baptism, and one of the best "definition" of salvation: unto a perfect man, unto the measure of the stature of the fulness of Christ". It also has marvelous Christology that many have never heard of, even in the church. Let us marvel at it, and be strengthened in our struggles.
